plan: Step 1 [IMPLEMENTATION]: Plumb quality.dofStrength into all 6 point-based system config interfaces and factories. Add `dofStrength?: number` to ParticleFieldConfig (particleField.ts:33), CrystalFieldConfig (crystalField.ts:19), FlowRibbonFieldConfig (flowRibbonField.ts:20), RibbonFieldConfig (ribbonField.ts:23), PointCloudConfig (pointCloud.ts:35), and TerrainHeightfieldConfig (terrainHeightfield.ts:15). In each system's init(), replace the hardcoded `uDofStrength: { value: 0.6 }` with `uDofStrength: { value: config.dofStrength ?? 0.6 }`. This is the core gap: quality.ts defines dofStrength as 0.0 (low), 0.4 (medium), 0.6 (high), but every system ignores it and hardcodes 0.6, meaning low-tier devices still compute full DoF in the shader for zero visual benefit.

Step 2 [IMPLEMENTATION]: Pass quality.dofStrength to each system factory call in main.ts (lines 109-154). Add `dofStrength: quality.dofStrength` to each createXxx() config object: createParticleField, createRibbonField, createPointCloud, createCrystalField, createFlowRibbonField, createTerrainHeightfield. This connects the quality tier computation to the actual shader uniform values.

Step 3 [IMPLEMENTATION]: Update the shaderRegistry.ts default (line 41) to document that 0.6 is the high-tier fallback, not a universal constant. Change the defaultValue comment or keep it as-is since individual systems now override via config. No functional change needed here — the registry default is only used when no explicit value is provided.

Step 4 [VERIFICATION ONLY — no code changes expected]: Confirm that bloom post-processing is already active for every mode in rotation. Verify: (a) composer.ts initComposer() creates EffectComposer with RenderPass + UnrealBloomPass when quality.enableBloom is true, (b) renderLoop.ts (lines 265-269) uses composer.render() when composer exists and falls back to direct renderer.render() otherwise, (c) modeManager.ts never touches the render pipeline or disables the composer during mode transitions. All three are already implemented correctly per the codebase audit.

Step 5 [VERIFICATION ONLY — no code changes expected]: Confirm that DoF focus uniforms (uFocusDistance) are already plumbed into all 6 active point-based systems with per-frame sinusoidal drift (baseFocus + sin(elapsed*0.0002)*0.5). All systems already have this. The only variation is pointCloud using baseFocus=3.5 for parametric shapes vs 5.0 elsewhere — this is intentional and correct.

Step 6 [EXPLICIT EXCLUSION NOTE]: Overlay systems (bezierCurveWeb.ts, constellationLines.ts) use THREE.LineSegments, not THREE.Points, and intentionally omit uDofStrength/uFocusDistance uniforms. The AC specifically lists 'particles, pointcloud, crystal, flowribbon' — line-based overlays are excluded by design since CoC bokeh blur is not meaningful for line geometry. No changes needed for these systems.

Step 7 [IMPLEMENTATION]: Update existing dof.test.ts tests. Ensure test T-078-09 (line 150) and T-078-22 (line 321) validate that systems initialized with medium-tier quality use dofStrength=0.4, and systems initialized with low-tier quality use dofStrength=0.0. Add a test case verifying that when no dofStrength config is provided, the fallback remains 0.6 (backward compatibility).

Step 8 [VALIDATION]: Run `npm run build` to confirm clean production build. Load the app and verify: no shader compile errors, no WebGL validation errors, no console errors. Visually confirm bloom is active across mode transitions. On a simulated low-tier profile (if dev tools allow), confirm DoF strength is 0 (no blur computation waste).
testing_recommendations: Verify each system's uDofStrength uniform value matches the quality tier: 0.0 for low, 0.4 for medium, 0.6 for high (or when no config provided)
Run existing dof.test.ts suite — all tests must pass with updated expectations for tier-aware dofStrength
Manual test: cycle through all modes (particles, pointcloud, crystal, flowribbon, terrain, ribbon) and confirm bloom remains active during every transition
Manual test: confirm no console errors (especially no shader compile errors or WebGL validation errors) across all modes
Performance test: on a low-tier device or throttled profile, confirm FPS is stable — dofStrength=0 should reduce shader cost vs the previous hardcoded 0.6
Regression test: verify overlay modes (bezierCurveWeb, constellationLines) still render correctly without DoF uniforms
Build validation: npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors from the new config properties
risks: Changing uDofStrength from 0.6 to 0.0 on low tier may produce a visible difference users notice — but this is the intended behavior per quality.ts design

If any system's draw() method also hardcodes dofStrength updates (not just init), those per-frame updates would override the config value — audit each draw() to ensure dofStrength is not re-hardcoded at runtime (current audit shows draw() only updates uFocusDistance, not uDofStrength, so this risk is low)

The fallback default of 0.6 in `config.dofStrength ?? 0.6` means any system created without explicit config still gets high-tier DoF — this preserves backward compatibility but means unconfigured test scenarios default to max blur
